key_insight: A SCAPS-1D + machine-learning workflow can efficiently rank 125 perovskite solar-cell architectures and uncover high-efficiency, lead-free double-perovskite designs.
engineering_relevance: The study provides practical layer-selection rules for optimizing ETL, absorber, and HTL choices in multilayer solar cells to boost power conversion efficiency.
method: Trained an ML model on a subset to predict PCE for unexplored stacks.
Used SHAP to identify the most influential descriptors and verify top candidates with full device simulations.
evidence_signal: Leave-One-Group-Out cross-validation showed strong rank-correlation performance, and the best verified devices reached 28.85% PCE (Cs2AgBiBr6) and 28.62% PCE (Cs2AgInBr6).
watch_item: The ML guidance depends on the simulated design space and model training set, so transfer to new materials or fabrication conditions still needs experimental validation.
paper_title: Multilayer Screening of Double and Conventional Perovskite Solar Cells Using SCAPS-1D and Machine Learning: Optimization of ETL, HTL, and Absorber for High-Efficiency Architectures
